Architecture Pattern: Layered: handlers → services → repositories; uses Tower middleware for cross-cutting concerns
Error Strategy: Custom error type implementing IntoResponse; map to proper HTTP status codes
Key Considerations: Axum is the most modern Rust web framework; built on tower/hyper; async-native; extractors for request parsing

Architecture Pattern: Create a shared Client instance; configure timeouts and connection pools; use middleware for retries
Error Strategy: Map reqwest::Error to domain errors; use .error_for_status() for HTTP error codes
Key Considerations: Reuse reqwest::Client (connection pooling); set default timeouts; use ureq for blocking contexts

Architecture Pattern: Derive Serialize/Deserialize on domain types; use #[serde(rename_all)] for convention mapping
Error Strategy: Propagate serde errors with ?; wrap in domain error type
Key Considerations: serde is the de facto standard; supports 30+ formats; use #[serde(default)] and #[serde(skip)] for control

Architecture Pattern: Compile-time query verification with sqlx::query!; connection pooling with PgPool; migrations with sqlx-cli
Error Strategy: Map sqlx::Error to domain errors; use transactions for atomicity
Key Considerations: SQLx: async, compile-time checked SQL. SeaORM: async ORM. Diesel: sync ORM with strong types

Architecture Pattern: Use derive API for type-safe args; use subcommands for complex CLIs; Builder API for dynamic args
Error Strategy: Use anyhow::Result for CLI apps; provide helpful error messages with .context()
Key Considerations: clap is the standard; dialoguer for interactive prompts; indicatif for progress bars; console for colors

Architecture Pattern: Configure worker_threads and max_blocking_threads based on workload; use tokio::select! for cancellation
Error Strategy: Ensure errors are Send + Sync for use across .await; use spawn_blocking for sync code
Key Considerations: Tokio is the dominant async runtime; async-std is the alternative; smol is minimal

Architecture Pattern: Use tracing::instrument for method-level spans; configure subscriber layers for formatting, filtering, and export
Error Strategy: Log errors at boundaries; include span context for correlation
Key Considerations: tracing is the standard; supports structured fields, spans, and OpenTelemetry export

Architecture Pattern: thiserror for library errors (typed); anyhow/eyre for application errors (erased); combine at layer boundaries
Error Strategy: Use #[from] for automatic conversion; .context() for human-readable chains
Key Considerations: thiserror for libraries; anyhow for binaries; color-eyre for CLI with backtraces

Architecture Pattern: rstest for parameterized tests; mockall for mocking traits; insta for snapshots; criterion for benchmarks
Error Strategy: Return Result from tests; test error cases explicitly; use assert_eq for clear diffs
Key Considerations: Built-in #[test] is powerful; add rstest for data-driven tests; proptest for fuzzy edge cases

Architecture Pattern: Load from env vars + config files with hierarchy; validate at startup; deserialize with serde
Error Strategy: Fail fast on invalid config; use .context() for which config field failed
Key Considerations: config crate supports TOML/YAML/JSON + env vars; dotenvy for .env files; envy for env-only

Architecture Pattern: Define services in .proto files; generate Rust code with tonic-build in build.rs; use tower middleware
Error Strategy: Map tonic::Status codes to domain errors; use metadata for auth
Key Considerations: tonic is the standard Rust gRPC framework; prost for protobuf serialization; health checks built-in

Architecture Pattern: Use chrono for full timezone support; use time crate as lighter alternative; use humantime for parsing durations
Error Strategy: Parse with error handling; handle timezone-aware and naive datetimes explicitly
Key Considerations: chrono is feature-rich; time is smaller and recommended for new projects since chrono had CVE in older versions

Architecture Pattern: Use UUIDv4 for random IDs; UUIDv7 for time-ordered IDs; ULID as an alternative sortable ID
Error Strategy: Wrap UUID in a newtype for type safety; implement From/Display
Key Considerations: UUIDv7 is preferred for database primary keys (sortable); v4 for general purpose

Architecture Pattern: Use Argon2id for new projects; spawn_blocking for async contexts; store PHC-format strings
Error Strategy: Never store plaintext; handle verify errors as authentication failures
Key Considerations: Argon2id is the OWASP recommendation; always use spawn_blocking as hashing is CPU-intensive

Architecture Pattern: Derive Validate on input structs; validate at API boundary before domain logic; return all errors at once
Error Strategy: Collect all validation errors; map to 400 Bad Request with field-level details
Key Considerations: validator: derive-based validation; garde: newer alternative; nutype: newtype validation at construction
